title: Frontend passes all 40 tests but DB contains zero rows — lock is never persisted
detail: The frontend integration suite reports 'POST /v1/lock creates lock with valid SR_0 and lock_id' as passed and 'lock is persisted in DB and visible via GET /v1/explorer/locks' as passed, yet the DB layer query returned an empty result set (stdout_excerpt is empty, zero rows). This is a classic cross-layer silent failure: the mock/stub backend that the Playwright tests hit is returning well-formed 200 responses and fabricated lock_ids/SR_0s without ever writing to the real database. The backend Rust test suite failed (exit_code=1), which confirms the real backend path is broken; the frontend is testing against a test-double that diverges from the real implementation. This means the lock sequence produces a plausible-looking lock_id (e.g. '0x049904a89315a6cd24fa92c01cc47aae1537b68a4a527b333ce127421cdfc4d4') that has no corresponding DB row and will never be submitted to L1.
evidence: frontend stdout: '[Lock] lock_id=0x049904a89315a6cd24fa92c01cc47aae1537b68a4a527b333ce127421cdfc4d4' — test claimed success
frontend stdout: '[Lock Persisted] lock_id=0xc60c47a77d3f7b5d1c6af06907c1df480266083f0468c9b6fcebb1b6060eaeb0, status=active' — persistence test claimed success
db layer stdout_excerpt is empty (zero rows returned from: SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1)
backend exit_code=1, no test execution output captured — real backend is broken
frontend stats: expected=40, unexpected=0 — all 40 tests passed against a backend that produces no DB writes

title: Backend test failure root cause hidden by output truncation — lock write path completely unverified
detail: The backend Rust test (`sequence_lock`) exited with code 1 after a 120-second run, but the entire stderr excerpt (2507 bytes truncated) contains only dependency compilation lines with zero test execution output. The stdout_excerpt is empty. This means: (1) the actual panic/assertion/error that caused exit_code=1 is invisible; (2) no log evidence exists for lock_id assignment, SR_0 computation (SHA3-256(QS_LOCK_V1 || ...)), or the `lockWithSR0` call to the L1Vault. The 120-second duration (timeout threshold) strongly suggests the test hung or panicked early rather than completing normally, which is consistent with the DB showing zero rows. Without the actual error, the silent-failure patterns from items 3, 4, and 5 in the priority list (unwrap_or_default, hex_to_bytes32_or_zero, Ok(()) swallowing errors) cannot be ruled out.
evidence: backend exit_code=1, duration_ms=120018 (exactly 120 seconds — timeout boundary)
backend stderr_excerpt ends at: 'Compiling iana-time-zone v0.1.64' — no test output follows
backend stdout_excerpt is empty — no PASSED/FAILED/panicked lines captured
stderr note: '[truncated 2507 bytes]' — actual failure message was cut off

title: L1 totalLocked state unverifiable — lock sequence cannot be confirmed end-to-end
detail: The L1 verification step against the canonical vault address (0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260) returned HTTP 522 from rpc.sepolia.org. Combined with the backend failure and the empty DB, there is no evidence that any `lockWithSR0` transaction was submitted to L1. The frontend test '[Explorer] totalLocks: 30 -> 31' only reflects the off-chain explorer counter, not the on-chain vault state. The sequence is therefore unverified at every real layer (backend, db, L1); only the frontend stub layer reports success.
evidence: l1 stderr: 'Error: HTTP error 522 with body: ... 522: Connection timed out' against cast call 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260
frontend stdout: '[Explorer] totalLocks: 30 -> 31' — off-chain counter only, no on-chain confirmation
db stdout_excerpt empty — no lock row exists that could have triggered an L1 submission
l1 exit_code=1 — no totalLocked value retrieved

title: pk_dilithium column presence unverified — Dilithium key storage regression possible
detail: The DB query explicitly selects `length(pk_dilithium)` to verify the 1952-byte Dilithium public key is stored with each lock. Because the query returned zero rows, it is impossible to know whether the INSERT code path even attempts to populate this column. If the backend is silently inserting a row without the pk_dilithium field (e.g. via unwrap_or_default on key generation), the column would be NULL or zero-length and the cryptographic binding between the consumer's lock and their post-quantum key would be broken. This cannot be confirmed or denied from the current evidence.
evidence: db command included: 'length(pk_dilithium)' in SELECT — intended to verify 1952-byte key
db stdout_excerpt is empty — zero rows returned, pk_dilithium length never validated
backend produces no output — no log line confirming Dilithium key generation or storage
